{{Infobox book
| name = The Engineer
| slug = lh-02-the-engineer
| precedes = [[The Knight (novel)|The Knight]]
| follows = [[The Captain (novel)|The Captain]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-lh-02-the-engineer.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The story follows the escalating conflict between Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar and the powerful, increasingly unstable android known as [[Starhammer]]. The narrative begins with [[Starhammer]] receiving a deceptive message from Priest of Night{{!}}the Priest of Night, claiming his wife [[Leilari]] is dead; however, a mysterious wizard reveals she is safe, sparing [[Starhammer]] from a path of grief-driven vengeance. Meanwhile, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar and the crew of the sentient starship *[[The Last Horizon]]* seek to recruit the exiled [[Karoshan]] [[Queen Shyrax]]. After a series of magical trials and duels, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax joins the crew as [[Commander]], bringing the group closer to full strength.

The crew's search for an [[Engineer]] leads them to [[Mell]] (operating under the alias [[Doctor Nova]]), a brilliant technician and lab-grown clone. Following a botched heist and her subsequent arrest by the Advocates on the frozen planet [[Carturus]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic and his crew launch a high-stakes prison break. During the rescue, the narrator—a mage with memories of past lives—uses rabbit summons to assist. Amidst the chaos, [[Starhammer]] arrives on [[Carturus]], leading to a violent confrontation where he is momentarily ambushed by the narrator and a mysterious wizard. Ultimately, [[Mell]] is rescued and officially joins *[[The Last Horizon]]*, eager to utilize its advanced [[Zenith]] technology.

As the crew attempts to repair their damaged ship, they navigate complex political waters involving the Advocates and the [[Iron Legion]]. The crew's power grows as the ship spirit, [[Horizon]], uses memory manifestation to fabricate a fleet of ships. However, tension rises as [[Starhammer]]’s '[[Evolution Engine]]' begins to trigger, enhancing his abilities while eroding his humanity. This instability peaks when [[Starhammer]] kills the [[Advocate]] [[Clockworker]] during a rescue of [[Leilari]], using the event to radicalize the Advocates under a new, proactive mandate. 

The conflict reaches a boiling point in a massive space battle where Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic’s fabricated fleet faces [[Starhammer]]'s forces. Although the crew manages to hold their own, [[Starhammer]] remains a terrifying force. He eventually launches a targeted strike against the crew, using a diversion involving [[Leilari]] to ambush them. In the ensuing chaos, [[Starhammer]]'s true nature as an android is exposed by [[Mell]] and [[Leilari]], causing him to descend into a destructive madness. 

The climax unfolds as [[Mell]] and the crew, aided by the awakened sentient ship [[Horizon]] and the [[Zenith Device]] [[Shadow Ark]], engage [[Starhammer]] in a desperate battle. In a final confrontation within [[Subspace]], the crew's combined technological and magical might overpowers [[Starhammer]], leading to his imprisonment. The novel concludes with the realization that the simultaneous activation of multiple [[Zenith Devices]] has signaled a massive shift in the galaxy, awakening ancient entities and technologies across the stars.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After [[Starhammer]] successfully rescues a civilian cruiser from living asteroids, he receives an urgent video message from Priest of Night{{!}}the Priest of Night, a prisoner who claims to have destroyed [[Starhammer]]'s home and killed his wife. The terrifying transmission is interrupted by a mysterious wizard who reveals that [[Starhammer]]'s wife, [[Leilari]], is actually safe and the bombs were neutralized. The video concludes with a warrior-rabbit killing Priest of Night{{!}}the Priest of Night, leaving [[Starhammer]] profoundly relieved to avoid a path of grief-driven vengeance.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|As the starship [[The Last Horizon]] prepares for the arrival of exiled [[Karoshan]] [[Queen Shyrax]], Captain and his crew discuss their damaged [[Subspace Drive]] and the potential for recruiting her to lead their fleets. After teleporting the Captain's father to safety and locking the chaotic [[Omega]] in a [[vault]], the crew reviews Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax's history of treason and her significant fleet of exile ships. The Captain plans to meet [[the Queen]] with his crew to negotiate her alliance, despite the risks posed by their current situation.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While resting in deep space, [[Queen Shyrax]] is hailed by the [[World Spirit]] of the legendary ship *[[The Last Horizon]]*, who sends representatives [[Sola Kalter]] and [[Raion Raithe]] to her bridge. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar subsequently arrives via magical transport to propose a partnership, prompting Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax to challenge him for command of the *The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship*. The chapter concludes with Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax accepting a magical trial proposed by the [[World Spirit]] to prove her worthiness for the crew.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax engages in a series of exhibition duels against the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] to test their abilities. After Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic easily overcomes her magic, the [[Archmage]] [[Melerius]] reveals that Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic possesses a divine connection to the [[Aether]], leading Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax to concede defeat. Following a high-intensity combat duel between Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax and [[Raion]], Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax accepts Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's invitation to join his crew.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic and the ship spirit [[Horizon]] successfully recruit [[Queen Shyrax]] to join the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] as its [[Commander]]. After Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic proves his credibility by revealing intimate knowledge of her past lives, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax agrees to the contract and the ship's mission. With five of the six crew positions now filled, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax immediately asks for the plan to find the final member.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]], operating under the alias [[Doctor Nova]], uses a swarm of [[Nova-Bots]] to hijack an [[Advocate]] vessel and surgically remove the [[Aethertech]] implants from the hero [[Moonmaster]]. The robbery goes awry when [[Moonmaster]]'s hidden communicator alerts more [[Advocate]] ships, forcing [[Mell]] to trigger a self-destruct sequence on her decoy robot to escape back to her secret warehouse. However, the Advocates track her to her laboratory and arrest her, leaving [[Mell]] with no choice but to surrender.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar learns that [[Mell]], a potential [[Engineer]] recruit, has been imprisoned by the Advocates on the frozen planet [[Carturus]] after an attempted theft. As Vallenar and [[Raion]] discuss the risks of rescuing her and the history of the powerful [[Starhammer]], [[Queen Shyrax]] and the [[Karoshan royal court]] arrive via subspace. The chapter concludes with Vallenar and Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax planning to repair the ship and escape the system before addressing future threats.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] convenes the [[High Council of the Advocates]] to discuss the disorganized movements of the [[Iron Legion]] and the potential threat of an [[Iron King]]. The council receives reports that a legendary ship, commanded by Varic Vallenar{{!}}Archmage Varic Vallenar, destroyed the [[Grand Hive]] near [[Dornoth IV]], prompting [[Starhammer]] to reflect on his own mysterious savior. After learning that the council member [[Blade Dancer]] has captured [[Doctor Nova]], [[Starhammer]] uses his temporal powers to fly secretly toward the prison world of [[Carturus]] to confront her.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While navigating the damaged ship *[[The Last Horizon]]* through [[Subspace]], the Captain struggles to maintain magical seals that hold the vessel together. The fleet is ambushed by a slipwraith, a creature that attempts to trap them in [[Subspace]], prompting [[Omega]] and [[Raion]] to engage the entity. Despite [[Omega]]'s sabotage and the intense physical strain on the Captain, the crew successfully drives off the creature and surfaces back into reality.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After emerging in the [[Scylon system]] near [[Advocate]] territory, the Captain and her crew prepare for a high-stakes prison break to rescue [[Mell]], a master [[Aether Technician]]. To avoid detection by the Advocates and the formidable [[Starhammer]], [[Horizon]] uses an advanced fabricator to create disguises for the team. The group plans to crash a captured shuttle onto the planet [[Carturus]] to infiltrate the prison and steal an [[Advocate ship]] for their escape.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|In a past life, the narrator works as a mage for hire for the [[Nebula Runners]] smuggling crew, tasked with guarding a valuable [[Temporal Prism]]. The mission turns violent when an [[Advocate]] named [[Steel Avalanche]] boards the ship, murders the captain, and engages in a brutal fight with the crew. After the narrator uses rabbit summons to force Avalanche into an escape pod, they encounter [[Mell]], a fugitive posing as '[[Doctor Nova]],' who offers them a new job.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola]], Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax, and the narrator crash a captured [[Lichborn]] shuttle onto the frozen planet of [[Carturus]] to reach an [[Advocate prison]]. After surviving planetary defenses and a crash landing, the group is ambushed by an [[Advocate]] ice wizard, whom the narrator defeats by summoning a powerful [[Thornfur Brawler]] rabbit from their hat. With the wizard captured and a path identified toward their target, [[Mell]], the group continues their mission while remaining wary of the powerful [[Starhammer]].}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After being imprisoned by the Advocates, [[Mell]] struggles with isolation and boredom until [[Starhammer]] visits her to offer a job and dismisses her concerns about his deteriorating mental state. Their confrontation is interrupted when a [[Karoshan]] [[Combat Artist]] breaches the cell wall amidst a chaotic battlefield of explosions and gunfire. The intruder seizes [[Mell]] and sprints away through the carnage, narrowly escaping a magical blast from an armored attacker.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] uses his [[Temporal Field]] to intervene as a [[Karoshan]] soldier attempts to kidnap [[Mell]], successfully overpowering the warrior and rescuing the girl. During the fight, he is harassed by a summoned lightning-rabbit and eventually discovers a mysterious, masked wizard controlling the battlefield via magic circle{{!}}magic circles. The chapter concludes with the wizard summoning multiple lightning-rabbits that strike [[Starhammer]] as he tries to protect [[Mell]].}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After successfully ambushing [[Starhammer]] with lightning rabbits, the narrator hesitates to deliver a killing blow due to memories of [[Mell]]'s connection to him. [[Starhammer]] escapes by activating his [[Subspace Drive]], prompting an arrival of [[Advocate]] ships and a chaotic skirmish involving [[Sola]], Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax, and mid-level [[Advocates]]. The narrator eventually infiltrates an [[Advocate]] shuttle, uses telekinesis to subdue the Karoshan forces{{!}}Karoshan soldiers, and asserts command over them as their Captain.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After failing to protect [[Mell]], [[Starhammer]] experiences a surge of power that activates his [[Evolution Engine]] for the fifth time. Meanwhile, aboard a shuttle fleeing the area, a wizard explains to a fearful [[Mell]] that he possesses memories of alternate lives and intends to recruit her as an engineer. The group eventually arrives at the bridge of [[The Last Horizon]], where they discover three captured [[Advocates]] and the wreckage of their ships.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After the crew captures three [[Advocate]] prisoners, the Captain uses magic to seal their powers and prevent them from revealing the ship's identity. Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax records a diplomatic message to an incoming [[Advocate]] fleet, claiming they are Karoshan forces{{!}}Karoshan soldiers to maintain their cover. Meanwhile, the engineer [[Mell]] is officially recruited to join the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] after expressing fascination with its advanced technology.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The crew gathers in the ship's ritual room to finalize their contracts, where each member reveals their personal mission or request. After initially appearing intimidated and indecisive, the new [[Engineer]], [[Mell]], impresses [[Queen Shyrax]] by requesting access to all the [[Zenith Devices]] to advance galactic technology. Following this display of spirit, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax agrees to accept [[Mell]] as the crew's [[Engineer]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After the crew reaches a consensus on the terms of [[Mell]]'s request, she officially joins the crew as the ship's [[Engineer]] through a magical contract with [[Horizon]]. [[Horizon]] provides a potential lead regarding the [[Zenith Colony]] on the planet [[Murrok]], prompting the Captain to agree to investigate despite the risks of entering the [[Free Worlds]]. The chapter concludes with [[Mell]] discovering the massive structural damage to the ship and demanding detailed schematics to begin repairs.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While watching a movie, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic reveals to [[Mell]] that his last name is Vallenar, a powerful corporation he fled. In response, [[Mell]] discloses her own past as a lab-grown clone created by [[Stellar Synthesis Labs]]. She explains that she eventually sabotaged the facility in retaliation for their neglect before leaving to start a new life.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola]] visits the weapons laboratory of [[The Last Horizon]] to decide how to spend 164,627 Units earned from defeating the [[Iron King]], hesitating between upgrading her armor or seeking a way to counter [[Starhammer]]'s temporal abilities. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic joins her, revealing his past lives and his fear of failing to prevent a catastrophic ending, before arranging for an expert technician to assist with her suit. [[Queen Shyrax]] arrives and takes charge of the maintenance, declaring [[Sola]]'s armor the perfect medium for her to perform a masterwork enhancement.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] holds a holographic conversation with his grandson, [[Jak]], the president of the [[Escalon Company]], where he mocks [[Jak]]'s weakness and refusal to take responsibility for his own problems. Following the call, [[Raion Raithe]] enters [[Omega]]'s room and mistakenly interprets the interaction as a moment of familial affection, much to [[Omega]]'s irritation. The chapter concludes with [[Omega]] feeling uncharacteristically wary of Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar, suspecting the captain may know him better than he knows himself.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While hiding behind [[Karoshan ships]], the narrator watches a tense diplomatic standoff between [[Queen Shyrax]]'s recorded message and the [[Advocate]] [[High Councilor]], [[Steel Avalanche]]. The narrator attempts a specialized pathfinding spell to locate the dangerous [[Starhammer]], but the magic produces a terrifying reaction that confirms he is hunting them. To avoid him while he recovers, the narrator and [[Horizon]] decide to pursue the lost [[Shadow Ark]] colony, which offers both a perfect hiding place and necessary materials for [[Horizon]]'s restoration.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Horizon]] utilizes her [[Engineer]] gift and [[Mell]]'s Technicians{{!}}Technician abilities to rapidly fabricate various sizes of [[Nova-Bots]] using materials harvested from asteroids. The group witnesses [[Horizon]]'s immense power as she begins constructing a fleet of starships by combining the crew's diverse magical and technical skills. Despite the impressive display, [[Mell]] expresses concern about the morality of building an army, while the narrator realizes that even this massive production may not be enough to face their upcoming threats.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While meditating within the [[Terminus Mundi]] spell, the Captain learns that [[Horizon]]'s magic is based on memory manifestation, allowing her to conjure magical body parts from hundreds of past inhabitants. [[Horizon]] explains that these manifestations can carry specific powers, such as hypnosis, provided a deep bond is formed with the subject. The conversation also reveals that while two active [[Zenith Devices]] are merely a coincidence, the simultaneous activation of three would be cause for concern.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After receiving a magical video message from her former students, the Captain discovers that [[Mariala Brechess]] and a group of mages are traveling through space to find her for instruction. Using [[pathfinding magic]], the Captain locates them in the [[Kallver System]] and decides to deviate from her course to rescue them. She plans to use a shuttle to intercept the students, potentially utilizing [[Horizon]]'s piloting abilities to complete the mission quickly.}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While navigating a shuttle toward the [[Gardener 315]] colony, the Captain manages [[Omega]]'s voracious eating habits and discusses their dwindling resources. Upon arrival, they encounter [[Advocates]] conducting ship searches, prompting [[Omega]] to propose a mission to infiltrate the colony and retrieve several people. After a tense exchange regarding his methods and priorities, [[Omega]] vanishes into [[Subspace]] to begin the task.}}
* '''Chapter 28:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After rescuing a group of students from a drifting life-pod, the Captain discovers that the [[Advocate]] Councilor [[Steel Avalanche]] has sabotaged a colony's water purification plant. To retaliate, the Captain uses the powerful staff [[Eurias]] to conjure a massive whirlpool of water to trap Avalanche. The chapter concludes with chaos aboard the Captain's ship as the students mistakenly attack [[Omega]].}}
* '''Chapter 29:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar and his crew arrive at the inhospitable planet [[Murrok]] to negotiate with its [[World Spirit]] for information regarding the [[Shadow Ark]]. After a tense encounter where [[Horizon]]'s bold personality nearly provokes the spirit, [[Murrok]] agrees to provide the location in exchange for the crew raiding the [[Advocate fortress]] to retrieve [[Horizon]]'s brother. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic remains deeply skeptical of the request, knowing that attacking the Advocates would bring dangerous consequences.}}
* '''Chapter 30:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After successfully locating the [[Shadow Ark]], the crew debates whether to assault [[Advocate headquarters]] or withdraw to avoid a direct conflict with [[Starhammer]]. Despite disagreements regarding the risks and the potential for the [[Shadow Ark]] to contact [[Starhammer]], the majority votes to leave the Advocates alone. The Captain decides to prioritize repairing the ship and pursuing other leads, ordering the crew to enter subspace to depart the [[Free Worlds]].}}
* '''Chapter 31:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After waking in the [[Advocate infirmary]], [[Starhammer]] discusses the kidnapping of [[Doctor Nova]] with [[Clockworker]] before being drawn by mysterious violet pulses to the facility's evidence containment. There, he encounters [[Shadow Ark]], a sentient [[Zenith Device]] that has escaped its containment unit. The artifact reveals it called [[Starhammer]] because of his connection to the 'The Last Horizon{{!}}Last Horizon' and insists that it is time to move.}}
* '''Chapter 32:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|As the ship's [[Nova-Bots]] work to repair [[The Last Horizon]], the Captain discusses his strained relationship with the new [[Engineer]], [[Mell]], and receives advice from [[Horizon]] on how to better integrate her into the crew. During a group workout in a newly fabricated gym, the crew discusses their dire situation: they lack the materials for full repairs and face hostility from the [[Galactic Union]], the Alliance, and the [[Vallenar Corporation]]. The Captain concludes that they cannot simply hide in [[Dark Space]] to wait out their repairs, as he senses an impending threat is approaching.}}
* '''Chapter 33:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic has a tense holographic negotiation with his father, [[Benri Vallenar]], who offers assistance in exchange for control over the crew and ship. Sensing an imminent threat through his intuition, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic prepares the *The Last Horizon{{!}}Last Horizon* for a massive ambush. Meanwhile, [[Starhammer]] leads a combined fleet of [[Advocates]], Karoshan{{!}}Karoshans, and the [[Galactic Union]] through the [[Shadow Ark]]'s [[Zenith Colony]] to launch a surprise attack.}}
* '''Chapter 34:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|As the Captain prepares for an imminent ambush, he reflects on the political landscape of the galaxy and his fears regarding the potential consequences of a battle with [[Starhammer]]. He discusses these anxieties with [[Horizon]], a [[Zenith Device]], while their fleet of [[Karoshan ships]] and asteroids forms a defensive fortress. The chapter concludes as the massive [[Shadow Ark]] emerges from [[Subspace]], prompting the crew to prepare for combat.}}
* '''Chapter 35:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] and his fleet emerge in the [[Thevellian System]] to find an unexpected, fortified enemy force led by Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar of [[The Last Horizon]]. After a tense holographic negotiation where [[Starhammer]] attempts to demand the return of [[Doctor Nova]], both sides agree to a ceasefire and retreat. However, the peace is shattered when unknown magical attacks destroy several [[Galactic Union]] ships, prompting [[Starhammer]] to order his fleet into combat positions.}}
* '''Chapter 36:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|As [[Solstice]] ships explode to force a conflict, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar leads [[The Last Horizon]] into battle against [[the Perfected]] using a fleet of newly fabricated ships and a massive magical ritual involving enchanted asteroids. Simultaneously, the mercenary [[Omega]] ambushes [[Starhammer]] in [[Subspace]], using a parasitic infection and a forced exit to drag him into a trap. Upon emerging into real space, [[Starhammer]] is surrounded by [[Horizon]]'s fleet and subsequently attacked by a mysterious soldier capable of moving within his [[Temporal Field]].}}
* '''Chapter 37:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola]] engages [[Starhammer]] within his [[Temporal Field]], using specialized weapons to wound him before she is killed by a surge of white light. Following her death, [[Starhammer]] is ambushed by [[Raion Raithe]], a [[Titan Knight]] who uses immense speed and Aetheric combat arts to batter him. As the battle concludes, [[Raion]] reveals his motive for the attack is revenge for a fallen friend before vanishing in a burst of blue light.}}
* '''Chapter 38:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|During a massive space battle, the protagonist uses the [[Mirror of Silence]] to multiply ships and defend against [[Galactic Union]] forces, while simultaneously managing threats from a massive dreadnought and powerful [[Advocates]]. After [[Raion]] defeats [[Starhammer]] in a brutal duel, the protagonist attempts to prevent [[Starhammer]] from using the [[Evolution Engine]] by coordinating a lethal strike with [[Mell]] and the ship [[Horizon]]. However, [[Shadow Ark]] intervenes to rescue the wounded [[Starhammer]], and despite the protagonist's attempt to destroy his transport ship, [[Ark]] successfully escapes into [[Subspace]].}}
* '''Chapter 39:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Queen Shyrax]] conducts a post-battle review, critiquing the crew's performance and addressing the Captain's perceived failures while emphasizing that the leadership failed to properly support the Captain's immense capabilities. [[Engineer]] [[Mell]] explains that [[Starhammer]] is undergoing dangerous evolutions via the [[Evolution Engine]], which enhances his abilities but erodes his personality. The chapter concludes with the Captain realizing that they must find a way to strip [[Starhammer]] of his momentum before he launches a preemptive strike.}}
* '''Chapter 40:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Leilari]] is being relocated to a civilian colony by [[Clockworker]] because [[Starhammer]]'s [[Evolution Engine]] has triggered a massive, unprecedented warp in the [[Aether]], threatening to alter his personality. During their transit, [[Starhammer]] arrives and destroys their vehicle to rescue [[Leilari]], leading to a confrontation with [[Clockworker]]. The encounter ends when [[Starhammer]] brutally kills [[Clockworker]] after accusing him of complicity in a terrorist plot.}}
* '''Chapter 41:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The crew of [[The Last Horizon]] gathers to discuss the growing threat of [[Starhammer]], concluding that they must kill him before he begins taking hostages to further his agenda. During the meeting, a debate arises regarding [[Starhammer's design]] and his protective impulses toward his creator, [[Mell]]. Ultimately, the Captain decides to forgo a direct assault on the Advocates in favor of spreading out to catch [[Starhammer]], while the group prepares for an inevitable confrontation.}}
* '''Chapter 42:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] addresses a massive gathering of sixty thousand [[Advocates]] to announce that the respected member [[Clockworker]] betrayed him and was killed during a confrontation involving the kidnapping of [[Starhammer]]'s wife. Using this betrayal as a catalyst, [[Starhammer]] declares a shift in his leadership philosophy, moving away from compromise and toward proactive intervention against threats like the rogue starship [[The Last Horizon]]. He calls upon the Advocates to follow his new mandate of removing those who cause harm, regardless of jurisdictional boundaries.}}
* '''Chapter 43:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Jak]] is held captive by the [[Nuren group]] and faces execution until a team of [[Advocates]] arrives to arrest him. The situation escalates when [[Omega]] appears, attacking the Advocates and intimidating the [[Nuren]] leader, Nafir. Ultimately, [[Omega]] transforms into ooze and sweeps [[Jak]] away into the shadows.}}
* '''Chapter 44:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion]] arrives on [[Nyron Seven]] to warn his friend [[Javik]] that the Advocates may attempt to kidnap him as a hostage. An [[Advocate]] pilot named [[Undermaker]] arrives, leading to a confrontation where [[Raion]] challenges him to a duel to test his character and combat skill. After a brief skirmish, [[Raion]] demonstrates his superior power by slicing [[Undermaker's ship]] in half, forcing the [[Advocate]] to surrender.}}
* '''Chapter 45:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] monitors their allies' movements across the galaxy, [[Starhammer]] prepares to launch a strike against them using the [[Zenith Device]] known as [[Shadow Ark]]. [[Starhammer]] travels through subspace with the intention of capturing [[Mell]] and neutralizing the Captain, equipped with powerful [[Orichalcum]] enchantments to resist magic. Upon arriving at his destination, he finds himself face-to-face with a mysterious figure who resembles the wizard [[Varic Vallenar]].}}
* '''Chapter 46:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Varic Vallenar]] confronts [[Starhammer]] using a magical decoy to demonstrate that the leader's obsession with security has left his wife, [[Leilari]], vulnerable. After successfully reuniting [[Leilari]] with [[Starhammer]] and proving his point, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic reveals that the entire encounter was a diversionary tactic. The chapter concludes with Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic and [[Mell]] launching a coordinated magical and technological ambush to capture [[Starhammer]] and [[Leilari]].}}
* '''Chapter 47:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After being betrayed by [[Doctor Nova]] and incapacitated by a cloud of microscopic [[Nova-Bots]], [[Starhammer]] uses [[Shadow Ark]] to track Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar's ship into [[Subspace]]. The [[Zenith Colony]] consumes the vessel, forcing Vallenar to teleport [[Mell]] to safety before engaging in a desperate battle against [[Starhammer]]. As the ship's defenses fail and [[Aether]] levels drop, Vallenar and a legion of [[Nova-Bots]] prepare for a final stand against the boarding [[Starhammer]].}}
* '''Chapter 48:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]] manifests on [[The Last Horizon]] to find herself separated from the crew, realizing she must protect the ship and avenge Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic. On the bridge, a narrator uses various firearms and [[Nova-Bots]] to fight [[Starhammer]], but the ship's defenses eventually fail. After [[Starhammer]] breaches the bridge and pins the narrator down, he realizes [[Mell]] is missing before deciding to take the narrator into custody instead of killing them.}}
* '''Chapter 49:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After being captured due to a mechanical delay during a heist, the [[Spellbinder]] is interrogated and punished by the Advocates for refusing to reveal [[Doctor Nova]]'s location. He is brought before a crowd where [[Starhammer]] systematically mutilates him to coerce information, eventually forcing the [[Spellbinder]] to reveal [[Mell]]'s whereabouts. Despite his sacrifice, [[Starhammer]] proceeds to execute him, marking the end of that life.}}
* '''Chapter 50:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|After gathering the scattered crew members, [[Mell]] joins her companions to plan a high-stakes mission to rescue the Captain from an [[Advocate]] stronghold. While [[Mell]] expresses doubt regarding the crew's certainty that the Captain is still alive, her teammates reveal they have various methods of confirming his survival. Following a confrontation with [[Queen Shyrax]] about her self-imposed limitations, [[Mell]] accepts the title of "[[Engineer]]" and agrees to join the combat mission by seeking out fabricators.}}
* '''Chapter 51:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|While guarding the prisoner [[Varic Vallenar]], [[Steel Avalanche]] begins to suspect that the wizard is secretly sabotaging his anti-magic cell. Following an explosion at the docks, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic successfully collapses the prison's magical defenses and uses [[Aetheric manipulation]] to overpower Avalanche in a physical confrontation. Despite his enchanted iron-and-crystal manacles{{!}}manacles, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic demonstrates immense power and ultimately uses telekinesis to toss the resisting Avalanche into a cell.}}
* '''Chapter 52:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|To protect his secret identity as an android, [[Starhammer]] uses his [[Subspace Drive]] to kill the technicians repairing him and attempts to frame the incident as an attack. [[Mell]] exposes his true nature via a hologram at the docks, prompting [[Starhammer]]'s wife, [[Leilari]], to broadcast his crimes to the [[Subline]]. Devastated and unhinged by the exposure, [[Starhammer]] turns his destructive powers on both his allies and enemies alike.}}
* '''Chapter 53:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]] uses decoy-bots to infiltrate an [[Advocate]] hangar, where she successfully awakens the sentient starship [[The Last Horizon]]. After [[Horizon]] summons a weakened [[Shadow Ark]], the group realizes that [[Starhammer]] is currently devastating the Advocates and [[Mell]]'s crew. To combat him, [[Horizon]] demands that [[Mell]] perform an emergency, high-speed repair of the ship's systems to prepare for battle.}}
* '''Chapter 54:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|During a desperate battle against the unstoppable [[Starhammer]], [[Raion]] attempts to summon a [[Divine Titan]], but the android intercepts it and destroys the [[Advocate headquarters]], destabilizing the colony. The crew manages to regroup aboard the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship, which is empowered by [[Horizon]] to pursue [[Starhammer]] into [[Subspace]]. In a final confrontation in the void of space, the ship's weapons overpower [[Starhammer]] and blind him, leading to a tense communication where Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Varic Vallenar delivers a final message.}}
* '''Chapter 55:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Following the imprisonment of [[Starhammer]], the protagonist discusses the nature of [[Zenith Devices]] with [[Horizon]] and [[Shadow Ark]], noting that their simultaneous activation could signal massive galactic change. Across the galaxy, various ancient entities and technologies—including a [[force-blade]], a talking plasma pistol, and the [[World Spirit of the Zenith Engine]]—begin to stir or awaken. Finally, [[Lutoch Terelle]], a predictive analyst, is contacted by the [[Zenith Processor]], which reveals it has been awakened by an event that has not yet occurred.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Queen Shyrax]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Varic Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Doctor Nova]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Steel Avalanche]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Iron King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Leilari]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Clockworker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Melerius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Jak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Undermaker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Captain (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Priest of Night]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Visiri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola Kalter]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shyrax the Third]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mariala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Angelwing]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Lemon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Rebel Queen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Jyrid]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Advocates]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Karoshan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Free Worlds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Galactic Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Iron Legion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Murrok]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Carturus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Zenith Colony]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Vallenar Corporation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Perfected]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Karosha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[D’Niss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Solstice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Advocate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Dornoth IV]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Titan Force]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Grand Hive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Advocate prison]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Advocate headquarters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Planetary Security (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Magic Tower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Karoshan Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Technicians]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Thundertails]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Karoshan forces]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Colony]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Alliance (The Last Horizon)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Evolution Engine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Temporal Field]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aetheric symbols]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Combat Art]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mirror of Silence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Cosmic Path]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Combat Artist]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Dance of a Burning World]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[pathfinding magic]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Lagomorph Contract]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[contract magic]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Last Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Zenith Starship]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Eurias]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Zenith Device]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace Drive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Zenith Devices]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[force-blade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Dragon’s Edge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[levitation ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Karoshan ships]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Temporal Prism]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|fabricators}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Advocate ship]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aethertech]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Lichborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aethril]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aether Technician]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Engineer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[World Spirit]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Zenith Era]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Trade]]}}

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
